ain: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Ainu.
ani: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Andi.
avo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Agavotaguerra.
inn: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Isinai.
ins: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Indian Sign Language.
invasion: noun. A military action consisting of a large armed force of one geopolitical entity entering territory controlled by another such entity,
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
nan: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Min Nan.
naos: noun. The inner part of an ancient Greek temple, containing a statue of the temple's deity and surrounded by a colonnaded portico; the
nos: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eastern Nisu.
nova: noun. Any sudden brightening of a previously inconspicuous star.
ons: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ono.
sain: verb. To make the sign of the cross on or over something or someone.
savin: noun. The evergreen shrub Juniperus sabina, endemic to Europe, which yields a medicinal oil.
son: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Songhay languages.
vain: adjective. Overly proud of oneself, especially concerning appearance; having a high opinion of one's own accomplishments with slight reason.
van: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Valman.
vas: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Vasavi.
via: noun. A main road or highway, especially in ancient Rome. (Mainly used in set phrases, below.)
vis: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Vishavan.
visa: noun. A permit to enter and leave a country, normally issued by the authorities of the country to be visited.
vision: noun. The sense or ability of sight.
